part3Update: 0.6.0-pilot and the deterministic-graph candidate artifacts (v2/v3/v4 chain) required ZERO new cross-release-hash exceptions -- every new identity is a first publication, every existing identity's hash is unchanged. Full scan passes using only pre-existing exact historical exceptions. See deterministic-graph-part3-completion.json.
remediationUpdate: The 5 newly-discovered mismatches recorded below have since been remediated by WORKOUT-IMMUT-001 and PEAK-POLICY-IMMUT-001 (see published-workout-immutability-remediation.json, peak-volume-policy-immutability-remediation.json, dependency-version-cascade-audit.json). cross-release-hash-exceptions.json's canonical direction for WORKOUT_DEFINITION/PEAK_VOLUME_BAND_POLICY/PUBLISHED_TEMPLATE_BUNDLE v1 now points at the restored original hash; the mutated historical releases are registered as anomalies (reverse of this report's original framing below, preserved as the discovery record). TEMPLATE_COMBINATION v1 (COMB-IMMUT-001) is unaffected and unchanged.
requirement: For every (documentType,key,version) artifact identity, all historical release manifests under artifacts/appsel-plan-catalog/ must pin exactly one contentHash, across all versioned artifact types plus published bundles.
majorFinding: Repository-wide scan (run for the first time) confirms the already-known TEMPLATE_COMBINATION/TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1 mismatch AND surfaces 5 additional, previously undetected historical defects of the same class (in-place mutation without a version bump), plus 1 fully-explained derived bundle-hash mismatch.

mismatchGroups.rootCause: catalog/workouts/easy-standard.v1.json edited in place (vocabulary reconciliation pass added allowedDistanceAccountingModes) without a version bump
catalog/policies/peak-volume-bands.v1.json edited in place (band-corroboration pass) without a version bump

mismatchGroups.status: derived, fully explained — not an independent defect
mismatchGroups.rootCause: bundle hash reflects the full resolved dependency closure; necessarily differs whenever any dependency above changes

confirmationMethod: Recomputed each artifact's content hash directly from today's catalog/ source using the project's own CatalogDocumentHasher/SystemTextJsonCanonicalSerializer via a throwaway console harness referencing PlanCatalog.Infrastructure directly (same code path as production). Every current-source hash matched the canonical (newer) value exactly.
remediationPerformed: None beyond registering explicit exceptions — no immutable release directory modified, no new v2 artifacts created for workouts/peak-volume-policy (out of scope for this task; flagged for follow-up decision).

exceptionMechanism.schema: one entry per (documentType,key,version) identity; canonicalContentHash + anomalies[] each with releaseVersion/observedContentHash/reason/supersedingRelease/auditReference; no wildcards

publishTimeGuard.preExistingSourceLevelGuard: PublishReadinessValidator.Validate already detects a same-build duplicate (documentType,key,version) with differing hashes WITHIN one source snapshot — proven for TemplateCombinationDefinition by the pre-existing CombinationImmutabilityTests.cs. Cannot catch drift against a PREVIOUSLY PUBLISHED release.
publishTimeGuard.gapClosed: Added CatalogPublisher.ValidateCrossReleaseHashConsistency: compares the new release's artifact+bundle hashes against every already-published release's manifest via new IPublishedArtifactRepository.ListReleaseVersions(); rejects with PUBLISH_HASH_MISMATCH_FOR_SAME_KEY_VERSION unless the exact identity+release+hash is a registered exception.
publishTimeGuard.codeReused: PUBLISH_HASH_MISMATCH_FOR_SAME_KEY_VERSION (same code as the existing source-level guard)

regressionVerification.build: dotnet build -c Release — succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors
regressionVerification.testSuite: dotnet test -c Release — 171/171 passed (up from 163 after Task A)

finalStatus: CROSS_RELEASE_HASH_CONSISTENCY: IMPLEMENTED — 5 additional pre-existing historical defects discovered and explicitly registered, not remediated further, flagged for follow-up
